results.title: New in Wolfram Language and Mathematica 15 - BigMarker.com
results.content: Register for Series  Log In. New in Wolfram Language and Mathematica 15. Version 15 of Wolfram Language and Mathematica is an impressive release for modern times. Providing a streamlined and precise way to represent and communicate ideas, Wolfram Language is proving to be a powerful tool for AIs, and it continues to be a powerful tool for humans using AIs. The new version features our built-in AI Assistant, immediately available in all Wolfram Notebooks with no configuration needed. You can also easily integrate your local AI environment with built-in Wolfram functions. Learn more about the latest tools plus lots of new core functionality in our New in Version 15 webinar series. You can read more about the latest features in Stephen Wolfram's release announcement. * Overview of Version 15: Useful AI and New Core Functionality. Wednesday, July 1, 2026 · 11:00 AM CDT. * Mathematical Computation for the Age of Quantum and AI. Thursday, July 23, 2026 · 11:00 AM CDT.

results.title: The saddest thing about Mathematica is that despite the incredible level of effo... | Hacker News
results.content: The saddest thing about Mathematica is that despite the incredible level of effort invested into it by some of the smartest people on the planet, it will *inevitably* be relegated to the dustbin of history. It's one thing to have a proprietary *product*, but for decades now proprietary languages have been a no-go for practically everyone. There is no other use for Wolfram Language scripts outside of Mathematica, which comes with a "license server". Can I embed Wolfram code into a C# app, publish it on GitHub, and have other people be able to use it without forking out $thousands for a Mathematica license? With people not learning Mathematica there never developed a demand for it - and because you couldn't expect to get your hands on a license it wasn't worth the investment both in time and money to learn mathematica, or worse yet, to make yourself dependent on it for getting work done.
